stories.body: Tehran has become the stage for vast, state-orchestrated mourning ceremonies for Ayatollah Ali Khamenei as authorities push revenge-laden messaging, ferry mourners between Shiite shrines in Iran and Iraq, drape the Mosalla complex in black-and-red banners, and circulate Mojtaba Khamenei’s clenched-fist symbol even as the wounded new leader stays out of sight, underscoring the uncertainty surrounding succession amid chants for retaliation against the U.S. and Israel.
stories.headline: Power struggle intensifies as millions join week-long funeral rites for Iran's slain supreme leader

stories.body: Interim president Delcy Rodríguez insists Venezuela’s institutions remain stable and mobilized despite rising anger over the June 24 twin earthquakes, yet grieving families describe chaotic morgues, unaffordable burials, and overwhelmed forensic services as official fatalities climb past 3,300 and international rescue teams wind down operations around devastated La Guaira.
stories.headline: Venezuela quake death toll nears 3,000 as Caracas defends aid response

stories.body: Super Typhoon Bavi’s 180 mph winds and 11-meter seas battered Guam, Rota, Tinian, and Saipan, toppling power systems, halting water wells, prompting coastal flood alerts, and forcing residents into shelters while U.S. military crews launched damage assessments as the enormous storm pushes west toward the Philippines yet keeps tropical-storm conditions over the islands overnight.
stories.headline: Super Typhoon Bavi slams Guam and the Northern Marianas with Category 5-force winds

stories.body: Marine Le Pen heads into a Paris appeals verdict that will determine whether her embezzlement conviction, four-year sentence with electronic tagging, and five-year ban from public office stand, a decision that could sideline the polling frontrunner and elevate Jordan Bardella just 10 months before France votes.
stories.headline: Le Pen awaits ruling that could decide her 2027 presidential run

stories.body: Independence Day events intended to showcase the United States’ 250th anniversary were marred by brutal heat, lightning evacuations on the National Mall, and President Donald Trump’s campaign-style rhetoric that drew cheers from loyalists and counter-marches from liberal activists and white supremacist groups alike across Washington and New York.
stories.headline: Heat and partisan rancor overshadow America’s 250th birthday celebrations

stories.body: With Washington already ringed by fences, heat advisories, and storms, hundreds of khaki-clad Patriot Front members rode the Metro for a flash-mob style march as President Trump promised the “most spectacular” rally on the fenced-off Mall, intensifying fears that the Freedom 250 festivities have blurred into a partisan campaign environment.
stories.headline: Patriot Front march heightens security worries during July 4 events in Washington

stories.body: A sprawling heat dome pushed temperatures above 105°F from New Jersey to New York, killing at least 19 people found in sweltering homes or cars, smashing records at LaGuardia and Atlantic City, and triggering severe storms whose high winds knocked out power to nearly a million customers even as officials warned that sticky nights would hamper recovery.
stories.headline: Relentless heat dome leaves residents dead and hundreds of thousands without power across the Midwest and Northeast

stories.body: Paul Pelosi, 86, could face a misdemeanor after Napa County deputies say he hit a parked car in Yountville, briefly stopped, and drove off without injury or intoxication, prompting DMV review of his driving fitness just four years after his DUI conviction and subsequent hammer attack in San Francisco.
stories.headline: Paul Pelosi faces possible charges over alleged Napa County hit-and-run

stories.body: Juanma Moreno began his third term as Andalusia’s president by defending a coalition with Vox that enshrines a “national priority” clause while insisting his so-called Andalusian way remains inclusive, praising immigrants’ contributions, and promising accessible government even as Vox leader Manuel Gavira becomes vice-president.
stories.headline: Moreno takes oath in Andalusia while trying to contain backlash over PP–Vox governing pact

stories.body: Alberto Núñez Feijóo’s call in Barcelona to “turn the page” on the independence crisis and possibly count on Junts votes for a censure motion against Pedro Sánchez has reopened deep rifts inside the Partido Popular, with Catalan leader Alejandro Fernández and figures like José María Aznar and Isabel Díaz Ayuso warning against any rapprochement even as Genoa headquarters frames cooperation as purely anti-Sánchez arithmetic.
stories.headline: PP hardliners resist Feijóo’s bid to ‘turn the page’ with Catalan nationalists

stories.body: Judge-ordered indictments of Guardia Civil director Mercedes González and operational chief DAO, alongside SEPI president Belén Gualda, add to a tally of 126 defendants tied to corruption probes touching the PSOE, yet Moncloa still expresses confidence even as opposition parties cite cases reaching former ministers, state companies, and Sánchez’s family to argue the administration is structurally compromised.
stories.headline: Fresh indictments against Guardia Civil leadership deepen Sánchez government’s legal woes

stories.body: Fire crews say the 2,200-hectare blaze in Les Gavarres is stabilized after intense weekend work and lifted evacuations for Vall Repòs and Les Cabanyes, yet inspections show at least 16 homes and industrial buildings badly damaged, 11 with structural issues, and authorities are keeping the massif closed while aerial teams tackle flare-ups and assess the largely protected natural area.
stories.headline: Les Gavarres wildfire stabilizes after evacuations, but inspections reveal widespread damage

stories.body: Meteorologists warn that a new heatwave beginning July 5 could last more than a week with Barcelona’s center likely topping 35–36°C, Collserola exceeding 35°C, sultry nights staying above 25°C, and Meteocat issuing escalating alerts across most of Catalonia while Protecció Civil activates Procicat to urge hydration and limited outdoor activity.
stories.headline: Catalonia braces for a second, longer heatwave with highs above 35 °C

stories.body: Mayor Jaume Collboni hailed Barcelona’s Grand Départ weekend as the city’s biggest sporting success since the 1992 Olympics after 120,000 fans packed Saturday’s 19.6 km team time trial and Sunday’s 168.5 km Tarragona-to-Montjuïc stage won by Isaac del Toro, even as scorching heat and closed streets tested transport networks and required elaborate crowd management.
stories.headline: Tour de France turns Barcelona into a global showcase despite transport strain

stories.body: From July 6 through late August, Metro line L4 trains will skip Verdaguer while an €8.6 million accessibility overhaul adds lifts, ramps, and raised platforms, and until September 6 riders must walk 300 meters at street level to switch between L1 and L3 at Catalunya station as TMB renovates the tunnel, replaces finishes, and removes enclosed asbestos without charging extra validations.
stories.headline: Barcelona shuts Verdaguer station and Catalunya transfer tunnel for summer upgrade works
